calendar.description: House Economic Development and Workforce Services Hearing
Senate Transportation, Public Utilities, Energy, and Technology Hearing

general_provisions: This bill modifies transportation, infrastructure, and housing provisions.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>defines terms;<hr><ltbullet>creates the State Housing Infrastructure Partnership Fund (fund) and provides for the use of fund money;<hr><ltbullet>creates the State Housing Infrastructure Partnership Board (infrastructure loan board) and describes the infrastructure loan board's membership and duties;<hr><ltbullet>requires the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity to provide staff support to the infrastructure loan board;<hr><ltbullet>authorizes the infrastructure loan board to make loans from the fund to qualifying political subdivisions to finance system improvements that will facilitate the construction of housing;<hr><ltbullet>establishes terms and requirements for infrastructure loans awarded by the infrastructure loan board;<hr><ltbullet>directs the state treasurer to complete a study that analyzes the economic effect of the infrastructure loan program;<hr><ltbullet>exempts conduct arising from the provision of affordable housing, if the housing benefits are provided to all qualified residents, from certain ethics requirements for public officers;<hr><ltbullet>modifies the membership of the Affordable Housing Infrastructure Grant Board (grant board) for the provision of affordable housing infrastructure grants;<hr><ltbullet>limits to owner-occupied the affordable housing for which public entities in certain counties may qualify for an affordable housing infrastructure grant;<hr><ltbullet>allows the grant board, in relation to awarding affordable housing infrastructure grants, to:<hr><ltbullet1>determine whether an owner-occupied dwelling qualifies as an affordable housing unit; and<hr><ltbullet1>waive certain preliminary cost estimate requirements;<hr><ltbullet>increases the maximum amount of bonds the Utah Department of Transportation (department) may authorize for affordable housing infrastructure grants;<hr><ltbullet>allows the department to use certain local option sales tax revenue to pay for affordable housing infrastructure grants, subject to repayment from bond proceeds;<hr><ltbullet>diverts a certain portion of revenue from the County of the First Class Highway Projects Fund for revitalization of a convention center;<hr><ltbullet>establishes expenditure requirements for revenue in the County of the First Class Highway Projects Fund;<hr><ltbullet>establishes requirements in relation to a public transit hub project for the Cottonwood Canyons;<hr><ltbullet>reduces the amount of revenue the department may allocate from the Transportation Investment Fund to pay for a certain roadway project;<hr><ltbullet>establishes the Convention Center Reserves Restricted Account (account);<hr><ltbullet>requires the Division of Finance to transfer a certain amount of revenue from the Transportation Investment Fund to the account;<hr><ltbullet>limits the use of money deposited into the account for revitalization of a convention center;<hr><ltbullet>establishes requirements for the department to coordinate and assist on certain projects, subject to availability of funding;<hr><ltbullet>allows state agencies to sell surplus real property at pre-entitlement appraised value to certain qualifying entities and defer payment for the purchase of such property;<hr><ltbullet>addresses the sale or disposal of real property by the department and other state agencies; and<hr><ltbullet>makes technical and conforming changes.
